title: Bruise
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: A bruise is skin discoloration from small blood vessels breaking under the skin.
Bruises can be painful and swollen and usually change color while healing.
Care can include rest, protection, cold packs early, and later gentle heat when appropriate.
Large unexplained bruises, bleeding risk, severe pain, neurovascular symptoms, fracture concern, or abuse concern require separate handling.
